# MCP Tool Reference

This page is auto-generated from the Bitbucket OpenAPI schemas and the
[default_mcp_config.yaml](../internal/config/default_mcp_config.yaml).

> **Descriptions marked with ✏️** are overridden by the default configuration to
> give the LLM more actionable guidance. Override any of them in a local
> `mcp_config.yaml` to tailor the agent's behaviour for your workspace.

---

## `bitbucket_addon` *(hidden by default)*

Manage Bitbucket Connect addons

---

## `bitbucket_branch-restrictions`

Manage Bitbucket branch restrictions

| Operation | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `listBranchRestrictions` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of all branch restrictions on the |
| `createABranchRestrictionRule` | `POST` | Creates a new branch restriction rule for a repository. |
| `getABranchRestrictionRule` | `GET` | Returns a specific branch restriction rule. |
| `updateABranchRestrictionRule` | `PUT` | Updates an existing branch restriction rule. |
| `deleteABranchRestrictionRule` | `DELETE` | Deletes an existing branch restriction rule. |

| `getTheBranchingModelConfigForARepository` | `GET` | Return the branching model configuration for a repository. The returned |

| `getTheBranchingModelConfigForAProject` | `GET` | Return the branching model configuration for a project. The returned |
| `updateTheBranchingModelConfigForAProject` | `PUT` | Update the branching model configuration for a project. |

## `bitbucket_commit-statuses`

Read or post CI/CD build statuses tied to individual commits.

| Operation | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `listCommitStatusesForACommit` | `GET` | Returns all CI/lint/coverage status checks for a commit SHA. Each entry has key (unique identifier), state (SUCCESSFUL/FAILED/INPROGRESS), name, url (link to the build), and description. Check this before approving or merging a PR. ✏️ |
| `createABuildStatusForACommit` | `POST` | Creates a new build status against the specified commit. |
| `getABuildStatusForACommit` | `GET` | Returns the specified build status for a commit. |
| `updateABuildStatusForACommit` | `PUT` | Used to update the current status of a build status object on the |
| `listCommitStatusesForAPullRequest` | `GET` | Returns the aggregate CI statuses across all commits in a pull request. Useful to confirm that all required checks have passed before merging. ✏️ |

## `bitbucket_commits`

Read commit history and compare branches or commits.

| Operation | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `getACommit` | `GET` | Returns the specified commit. |
| `approveACommit` | `POST` | Approve the specified commit as the authenticated user. |
| `unapproveACommit` | `DELETE` | Redact the authenticated user's approval of the specified commit. |
| `listACommitsComments` | `GET` | Returns the commit's comments. |
| `createCommentForACommit` | `POST` | Creates new comment on the specified commit. |
| `getACommitComment` | `GET` | Returns the specified commit comment. |
| `updateACommitComment` | `PUT` | Used to update the contents of a comment. Only the content of the comment can be updated. |
| `deleteACommitComment` | `DELETE` | Deletes the specified commit comment. |
| `listCommits` | `GET` | These are the repository's commits. They are paginated and returned |
| `listCommitsWithIncludeexclude` | `POST` | Identical to `GET /repositories/{workspace}/{repo_slug}/commits`, |
| `listCommitsForRevision` | `GET` | Lists commits reachable from a branch, tag, or SHA. Pass the branch name or full SHA as the `revision` path parameter. Use `include` to add refs and `exclude` to stop at a base ref (e.g., exclude=main). ✏️ |
| `listCommitsForRevisionUsingIncludeexclude` | `POST` | Identical to `GET /repositories/{workspace}/{repo_slug}/commits/{revision}`, |
| `compareTwoCommits` | `GET` | Returns the diff between two refs (branch names, tags, or commit SHAs). spec parameter is "<new>..<old>" or "<new>...<old>" for three-dot diff. Returns raw unified diff text. ✏️ |
| `compareTwoCommitDiffStats` | `GET` | Returns a file-level summary (files changed, lines added/removed) for the diff between two refs. Cheaper than compareTwoCommits when you only need to know which files changed, not their contents. ✏️ |
| `getTheCommonAncestorBetweenTwoCommits` | `GET` | Returns the best common ancestor between two commits, specified in a revspec |
| `getAPatchForTwoCommits` | `GET` | Produces a raw patch for a single commit (diffed against its first |

## `bitbucket_deployments`

Track deployments across environments (staging, production, etc.).

| Operation | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `listRepositoryDeployKeys` | `GET` | Returns all deploy-keys belonging to a repository. |
| `addARepositoryDeployKey` | `POST` | Create a new deploy key in a repository. Note: If authenticating a deploy key |
| `getARepositoryDeployKey` | `GET` | Returns the deploy key belonging to a specific key. |
| `updateARepositoryDeployKey` | `PUT` | Create a new deploy key in a repository. |
| `deleteARepositoryDeployKey` | `DELETE` | This deletes a deploy key from a repository. |
| `getDeploymentsForRepository` | `GET` | Find deployments |
| `getDeploymentForRepository` | `GET` | Retrieve a deployment |
| `getEnvironmentsForRepository` | `GET` | Find environments |
| `createEnvironment` | `POST` | Create an environment. |
| `getEnvironmentForRepository` | `GET` | Retrieve an environment |
| `deleteEnvironmentForRepository` | `DELETE` | Delete an environment |
| `updateEnvironmentForRepository` | `POST` | Update an environment |
| `listProjectDeployKeys` | `GET` | Returns all deploy keys belonging to a project. |
| `createAProjectDeployKey` | `POST` | Create a new deploy key in a project. |
| `getAProjectDeployKey` | `GET` | Returns the deploy key belonging to a specific key ID. |
| `deleteADeployKeyFromAProject` | `DELETE` | This deletes a deploy key from a project. |

## `bitbucket_downloads`

Manage Bitbucket repository downloads

| Operation | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `listDownloadArtifacts` | `GET` | Returns a list of download links associated with the repository. |
| `uploadADownloadArtifact` | `POST` | Upload new download artifacts. |
| `getADownloadArtifactLink` | `GET` | Return a redirect to the contents of a download artifact. |
| `deleteADownloadArtifact` | `DELETE` | Deletes the specified download artifact from the repository. |

## `bitbucket_issues`

Manage the issue tracker for a Bitbucket repository.

| Operation | Method | Description |

| `listComponents` | `GET` | Returns the components that have been defined in the issue tracker. |
| `getAComponentForIssues` | `GET` | Returns the specified issue tracker component object. |
| `listIssues` | `GET` | Lists issues in the repository tracker. Filter with the q parameter: ✏️ |
| `createAnIssue` | `POST` | Creates a new issue. Required body fields: title. Optional: content.raw (markdown body), kind ("bug", "enhancement", "proposal", "task"), priority ("trivial", "minor", "major", "critical", "blocker"), assignee.uuid. ✏️ |
| `exportIssues` | `POST` | A POST request to this endpoint initiates a new background celery task that archives the repo's issues. |
| `checkIssueExportStatus` | `GET` | This endpoint is used to poll for the progress of an issue export |
| `checkIssueImportStatus` | `GET` | When using GET, this endpoint reports the status of the current import task. |
| `importIssues` | `POST` | A POST request to this endpoint will import the zip file given by the archive parameter into the repository. All |
| `getAnIssue` | `GET` | Returns the specified issue. |
| `updateAnIssue` | `PUT` | Modifies the issue. |
| `deleteAnIssue` | `DELETE` | Deletes the specified issue. This requires write access to the |
| `listAttachmentsForAnIssue` | `GET` | Returns all attachments for this issue. |
| `uploadAnAttachmentToAnIssue` | `POST` | Upload new issue attachments. |
| `getAttachmentForAnIssue` | `GET` | Returns the contents of the specified file attachment. |
| `deleteAnAttachmentForAnIssue` | `DELETE` | Deletes an attachment. |
| `listChangesOnAnIssue` | `GET` | Returns the list of all changes that have been made to the specified |
| `modifyTheStateOfAnIssue` | `POST` | Makes a change to the specified issue. |
| `getIssueChangeObject` | `GET` | Returns the specified issue change object. |
| `listCommentsOnAnIssue` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of all comments that were made on the |
| `createACommentOnAnIssue` | `POST` | Creates a new issue comment. |
| `getACommentOnAnIssue` | `GET` | Returns the specified issue comment object. |
| `updateACommentOnAnIssue` | `PUT` | Updates the content of the specified issue comment. Note that only |
| `deleteACommentOnAnIssue` | `DELETE` | Deletes the specified comment. |
| `checkIfCurrentUserVotedForAnIssue` | `GET` | Check whether the authenticated user has voted for this issue. |
| `voteForAnIssue` | `PUT` | Vote for this issue. |
| `removeVoteForAnIssue` | `DELETE` | Retract your vote. |
| `checkIfCurrentUserIsWatchingAIssue` | `GET` | Indicated whether or not the authenticated user is watching this |
| `watchAnIssue` | `PUT` | Start watching this issue. |
| `stopWatchingAnIssue` | `DELETE` | Stop watching this issue. |
| `listMilestones` | `GET` | Returns the milestones that have been defined in the issue tracker. |
| `getAMilestone` | `GET` | Returns the specified issue tracker milestone object. |
| `listDefinedVersionsForIssues` | `GET` | Returns the versions that have been defined in the issue tracker. |
| `getADefinedVersionForIssues` | `GET` | Returns the specified issue tracker version object. |

## `bitbucket_pipelines`

Inspect and trigger Bitbucket Pipelines (CI/CD).
Debugging a failing build:
  1. getPipelinesForRepository       — List recent runs; note the pipeline UUID.
  2. getPipelineForRepository        — Get state and result of a specific run.
  3. getPipelineStepsForRepository   — List steps; identify which step failed.
  4. getPipelineStepLogForRepository — Read the raw log for the failed step.

| Operation | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `getDeploymentVariables` | `GET` | Find deployment environment level variables. |
| `createDeploymentVariable` | `POST` | Create a deployment environment level variable. |
| `updateDeploymentVariable` | `PUT` | Update a deployment environment level variable. |
| `deleteDeploymentVariable` | `DELETE` | Delete a deployment environment level variable. |
| `getPipelinesForRepository` | `GET` | Lists recent pipeline runs sorted by creation time (newest first). Each entry includes the UUID, state (PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED), result (SUCCESSFUL/FAILED/ERROR/STOPPED), trigger type, and branch. Use UUID in subsequent step and log calls. ✏️ |
| `createPipelineForRepository` | `POST` | Triggers a new pipeline run. Specify target.ref_type="branch" and target.ref_name="<branch>" to run on a specific branch. Optionally set target.selector.type="custom" and target.selector.pattern to target a custom pipeline defined in bitbucket-pipelines.yml. ✏️ |
| `getRepositoryPipelineCaches` | `GET` | Retrieve the repository pipelines caches. |
| `deleteRepositoryPipelineCaches` | `DELETE` | Delete repository cache versions by name. |
| `deleteRepositoryPipelineCache` | `DELETE` | Delete a repository cache. |
| `getRepositoryPipelineCacheContentURI` | `GET` | Retrieve the URI of the content of the specified cache. |
| `getRepositoryRunners` | `GET` | Retrieve repository runners. |
| `createRepositoryRunner` | `POST` | Create repository runner. |
| `getRepositoryRunner` | `GET` | Retrieve repository runner by uuid. |
| `updateRepositoryRunner` | `PUT` | Update repository runner. |
| `deleteRepositoryRunner` | `DELETE` | Delete repository runner by uuid. |
| `getPipelineForRepository` | `GET` | Retrieve a specified pipeline |
| `getPipelineStepsForRepository` | `GET` | Lists all steps of a pipeline run. Each step has a UUID, name, state, and result. Identify the failed step by result=FAILED, then pass its UUID to getPipelineStepLogForRepository to read the log. ✏️ |
| `getPipelineStepForRepository` | `GET` | Retrieve a given step of a pipeline. |
| `getPipelineStepLogForRepository` | `GET` | Returns the raw stdout/stderr log for a pipeline step as plain text. This is the primary way to diagnose build failures. The response is large text — look for "ERROR", "FAILED", or exception stack traces. ✏️ |
| `getPipelineContainerLog` | `GET` | Retrieve the log file for a build container or service container. |
| `getPipelineTestReports` | `GET` | Get a summary of test reports for a given step of a pipeline. |
| `getPipelineTestReportTestCases` | `GET` | Get test cases for a given step of a pipeline. |
| `getPipelineTestReportTestCaseReasons` | `GET` | Get test case reasons (output) for a given test case in a step of a pipeline. |
| `stopPipeline` | `POST` | Signal the stop of a pipeline and all of its steps that not have completed yet. |
| `getRepositoryPipelineConfig` | `GET` | Retrieve the repository pipelines configuration. |
| `updateRepositoryPipelineConfig` | `PUT` | Update the pipelines configuration for a repository. |
| `updateRepositoryBuildNumber` | `PUT` | Update the next build number that should be assigned to a pipeline. The next build number that will be configured has to be strictly higher than the current latest build number for this repository. |
| `getRepositoryPipelineSchedules` | `GET` | Retrieve the configured schedules for the given repository. |
| `createRepositoryPipelineSchedule` | `POST` | Create a schedule for the given repository. |
| `getRepositoryPipelineSchedule` | `GET` | Retrieve a schedule by its UUID. |
| `updateRepositoryPipelineSchedule` | `PUT` | Update a schedule. |
| `deleteRepositoryPipelineSchedule` | `DELETE` | Delete a schedule. |
| `getRepositoryPipelineScheduleExecutions` | `GET` | Retrieve the executions of a given schedule. |
| `getRepositoryPipelineSshKeyPair` | `GET` | Retrieve the repository SSH key pair excluding the SSH private key. The private key is a write only field and will never be exposed in the logs or the REST API. |
| `updateRepositoryPipelineKeyPair` | `PUT` | Create or update the repository SSH key pair. The private key will be set as a default SSH identity in your build container. |
| `deleteRepositoryPipelineKeyPair` | `DELETE` | Delete the repository SSH key pair. |
| `getRepositoryPipelineKnownHosts` | `GET` | Find repository level known hosts. |
| `createRepositoryPipelineKnownHost` | `POST` | Create a repository level known host. |
| `getRepositoryPipelineKnownHost` | `GET` | Retrieve a repository level known host. |
| `updateRepositoryPipelineKnownHost` | `PUT` | Update a repository level known host. |
| `deleteRepositoryPipelineKnownHost` | `DELETE` | Delete a repository level known host. |
| `getRepositoryPipelineVariables` | `GET` | Find repository level variables. |
| `createRepositoryPipelineVariable` | `POST` | Create a repository level variable. |
| `getRepositoryPipelineVariable` | `GET` | Retrieve a repository level variable. |
| `updateRepositoryPipelineVariable` | `PUT` | Update a repository level variable. |
| `deleteRepositoryPipelineVariable` | `DELETE` | Delete a repository level variable. |
| `getPipelineVariablesForTeam` | `GET` | Find account level variables. |
| `createPipelineVariableForTeam` | `POST` | Create an account level variable. |
| `getPipelineVariableForTeam` | `GET` | Retrieve a team level variable. |
| `updatePipelineVariableForTeam` | `PUT` | Update a team level variable. |
| `deletePipelineVariableForTeam` | `DELETE` | Delete a team level variable. |
| `getPipelineVariablesForUser` | `GET` | Find user level variables. |
| `createPipelineVariableForUser` | `POST` | Create a user level variable. |
| `getPipelineVariableForUser` | `GET` | Retrieve a user level variable. |
| `updatePipelineVariableForUser` | `PUT` | Update a user level variable. |
| `deletePipelineVariableForUser` | `DELETE` | Delete an account level variable. |
| `getOIDCConfiguration` | `GET` | This is part of OpenID Connect for Pipelines, see https://support.atlassian.com/bitbucket-cloud/docs/integrate-pipelines-with-resource-servers-using-oidc/ |
| `getOIDCKeys` | `GET` | This is part of OpenID Connect for Pipelines, see https://support.atlassian.com/bitbucket-cloud/docs/integrate-pipelines-with-resource-servers-using-oidc/ |
| `getWorkspaceRunners` | `GET` | Retrieve workspace runners. |
| `createWorkspaceRunner` | `POST` | Create workspace runner. |
| `getWorkspaceRunner` | `GET` | Get workspace runner by uuid. |
| `updateWorkspaceRunner` | `PUT` | Update workspace runner. |
| `deleteWorkspaceRunner` | `DELETE` | Delete workspace runner by uuid. |
| `getPipelineVariablesForWorkspace` | `GET` | Find workspace level variables. |
| `createPipelineVariableForWorkspace` | `POST` | Create a workspace level variable. |
| `getPipelineVariableForWorkspace` | `GET` | Retrieve a workspace level variable. |
| `updatePipelineVariableForWorkspace` | `PUT` | Update a workspace level variable. |
| `deletePipelineVariableForWorkspace` | `DELETE` | Delete a workspace level variable. |

---

## `bitbucket_pr`

Manage pull requests in a Bitbucket repository.
Typical workflow:
  1. listPullRequests           — Find open PRs; note the pull_request_id.
  2. getAPullRequest            — Read title, description, reviewers, state.
  3. listCommentsOnAPullRequest — Read existing review comments.
  4. listChangesInAPullRequest  — See which files changed.
  5. createACommentOnAPullRequest — Post a review comment.
  6. approveAPullRequest / requestChangesForAPullRequest — Submit review.
  7. mergeAPullRequest          — Merge when all checks pass.

| Operation | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `getPullrequestsForCommit` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of all pull requests as part of which this commit was reviewed. Pull Request Commit Links app must be installed first before using this API; installation automatically occurs when 'Go to pull request' is clicked from the web interface for a commit's details. |
| `listDefaultReviewers` | `GET` | Returns the repository's default reviewers. |
| `getADefaultReviewer` | `GET` | Returns the specified reviewer. |
| `addAUserToTheDefaultReviewers` | `PUT` | Adds the specified user to the repository's list of default |
| `removeAUserFromTheDefaultReviewers` | `DELETE` | Removes a default reviewer from the repository. |
| `listEffectiveDefaultReviewers` | `GET` | Returns the repository's effective default reviewers. This includes both default |
| `listPullRequests` | `GET` | Returns open (or filtered) pull requests for the repository. Always call this first to get the pull_request_id before reading PR details or posting comments. Filter by state with ?q=state="OPEN" or ?q=state="MERGED". ✏️ |
| `createAPullRequest` | `POST` | Opens a new pull request. Required body fields: title, source.branch.name, destination.branch.name. Optional: description, reviewers (array of {uuid} objects), close_source_branch. ✏️ |
| `listAPullRequestActivityLog` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of the pull request's activity log. |
| `getAPullRequest` | `GET` | Returns full details of a single pull request: title, description, source/destination branches, reviewers, state, and task count. Requires pull_request_id from listPullRequests. ✏️ |
| `updateAPullRequest` | `PUT` | Mutates the specified pull request. |
| `getRepositoriesPullrequestsActivity` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of the pull request's activity log. |
| `approveAPullRequest` | `POST` | Approves the pull request as the authenticated user. No body required. Use requestChangesForAPullRequest instead if you want to block the merge. ✏️ |
| `unapproveAPullRequest` | `DELETE` | Redact the authenticated user's approval of the specified pull |
| `listCommentsOnAPullRequest` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of the pull request's comments. |
| `createACommentOnAPullRequest` | `POST` | Posts a review comment on the pull request. To anchor a comment to a specific file line, include inline.path (file path), inline.from (start line, optional), and inline.to (line number). ✏️ |
| `getACommentOnAPullRequest` | `GET` | Returns a specific pull request comment. |
| `updateACommentOnAPullRequest` | `PUT` | Updates a specific pull request comment. |
| `deleteACommentOnAPullRequest` | `DELETE` | Deletes a specific pull request comment. |
| `resolveACommentThread` | `POST` | Resolve a comment thread |
| `reopenACommentThread` | `DELETE` | Reopen a comment thread |
| `listCommitsOnAPullRequest` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of the pull request's commits. |
| `declineAPullRequest` | `POST` | Declines the pull request. |
| `listChangesInAPullRequest` | `GET` | Returns the list of files changed in the pull request with their change type (added, modified, removed). Use this to understand the scope of changes before reading individual file diffs. ✏️ |
| `getTheDiffStatForAPullRequest` | `GET` | Redirects to the [repository diffstat](/cloud/bitbucket/rest/api-group-commits/#api-repositories-workspace-repo-slug-diffstat-spec-get) |
| `mergeAPullRequest` | `POST` | Merges the pull request. Common merge strategies: merge_commit, squash, fast_forward. Requires all required approvals and passing builds. Confirm with listCommitStatusesForAPullRequest first. ✏️ |
| `getTheMergeTaskStatusForAPullRequest` | `GET` | When merging a pull request takes too long, the client receives a |
| `getThePatchForAPullRequest` | `GET` | Redirects to the [repository patch](/cloud/bitbucket/rest/api-group-commits/#api-repositories-workspace-repo-slug-patch-spec-get) |
| `requestChangesForAPullRequest` | `POST` | Marks the pull request as needing changes from the author. This blocks merge until the change request is resolved or removed. ✏️ |
| `removeChangeRequestForAPullRequest` | `DELETE` | Remove change request for a pull request |
| `listCommitStatusesForAPullRequest` | `GET` | Returns all statuses (e.g. build results) for the given pull |
| `listTasksOnAPullRequest` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of the pull request's tasks. |
| `createATaskOnAPullRequest` | `POST` | Creates a new pull request task. |
| `getATaskOnAPullRequest` | `GET` | Returns a specific pull request task. |
| `updateATaskOnAPullRequest` | `PUT` | Updates a specific pull request task. |
| `deleteATaskOnAPullRequest` | `DELETE` | Deletes a specific pull request task. |
| `listWorkspacePullRequestsForAUser` | `GET` | Returns all workspace pull requests authored by the specified user. |

---

## `bitbucket_projects`

Manage Bitbucket projects

| Operation | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `listProjectsInAWorkspace` | `GET` | Returns the list of projects in this workspace. |
| `createAProjectInAWorkspace` | `POST` | Creates a new project. |
| `getAProjectForAWorkspace` | `GET` | Returns the requested project. |
| `updateAProjectForAWorkspace` | `PUT` | Since this endpoint can be used to both update and to create a |
| `deleteAProjectForAWorkspace` | `DELETE` | Deletes this project. This is an irreversible operation. |
| `listTheDefaultReviewersInAProject` | `GET` | Return a list of all default reviewers for a project. This is a list of users that will be added as default |
| `getWorkspacesProjectsDefault-Reviewers` | `GET` | Returns the specified default reviewer. |
| `addTheSpecificUserAsADefaultReviewerForTheProject` | `PUT` | Adds the specified user to the project's list of default reviewers. The method is |
| `removeTheSpecificUserFromTheProjectsDefaultReviewers` | `DELETE` | Removes a default reviewer from the project. |
| `listExplicitGroupPermissionsForAProject` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of explicit group permissions for the given project. |
| `getAnExplicitGroupPermissionForAProject` | `GET` | Returns the group permission for a given group and project. |
| `updateAnExplicitGroupPermissionForAProject` | `PUT` | Updates the group permission, or grants a new permission if one does not already exist. |
| `deleteAnExplicitGroupPermissionForAProject` | `DELETE` | Deletes the project group permission between the requested project and group, if one exists. |
| `listExplicitUserPermissionsForAProject` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of explicit user permissions for the given project. |
| `getAnExplicitUserPermissionForAProject` | `GET` | Returns the explicit user permission for a given user and project. |
| `updateAnExplicitUserPermissionForAProject` | `PUT` | Updates the explicit user permission for a given user and project. The selected |
| `deleteAnExplicitUserPermissionForAProject` | `DELETE` | Deletes the project user permission between the requested project and user, if one exists. |

## `bitbucket_properties` *(hidden by default)*

Manage Bitbucket application properties

---

## `bitbucket_refs`

Manage branches and tags in a Bitbucket repository.

| Operation | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `listBranchesAndTags` | `GET` | Returns the branches and tags in the repository. |
| `listOpenBranches` | `GET` | Lists all branches in the repository. Filter by name with ?q=name~"feature". Each entry includes the branch name and the latest commit hash (target.hash). Use this before createAPullRequest to verify the source branch exists. ✏️ |
| `createABranch` | `POST` | Creates a new branch. Required body fields: name (branch name) and target.hash (the commit SHA or an existing branch name to branch from). ✏️ |
| `getABranch` | `GET` | Returns a branch object within the specified repository. |
| `deleteABranch` | `DELETE` | Delete a branch in the specified repository. |
| `listTags` | `GET` | Returns the tags in the repository. |
| `createATag` | `POST` | Creates a new annotated tag in the specified repository. |
| `getATag` | `GET` | Returns the specified tag. |
| `deleteATag` | `DELETE` | Delete a tag in the specified repository. |

---

## `bitbucket_reports`

Manage Bitbucket commit reports and annotations

| Operation | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `getReportsForCommit` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of Reports linked to this commit. |
| `getReport` | `GET` | Returns a single Report matching the provided ID. |
| `createOrUpdateReport` | `PUT` | Creates or updates a report for the specified commit. |
| `deleteReport` | `DELETE` | Deletes a single Report matching the provided ID. |
| `getAnnotationsForReport` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of Annotations for a specified report. |
| `bulkCreateOrUpdateAnnotations` | `POST` | Bulk upload of annotations. |
| `getAnnotation` | `GET` | Returns a single Annotation matching the provided ID. |
| `createOrUpdateAnnotation` | `PUT` | Creates or updates an individual annotation for the specified report. |
| `deleteAnnotation` | `DELETE` | Deletes a single Annotation matching the provided ID. |

---

## `bitbucket_repos`

Browse and manage Bitbucket repositories and read source files.
Common operations:
  - listRepositoriesInAWorkspace — Find repos by name or project.
  - getARepository               — Read repo metadata (main branch, links).
  - getFileOrDirectoryContents   — Read source files or list a directory.
  - createARepository            — Create a new repo.

| Operation | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `listPublicRepositories` | `GET` | **This endpoint is deprecated. Please use the |
| `listRepositoriesInAWorkspace` | `GET` | Lists all repositories the authenticated user can access in the workspace. Filter with ?q=name~"keyword". Returns slug, full_name, main_branch, description, and links for each repo. ✏️ |
| `getARepository` | `GET` | Returns the object describing this repository. |
| `createARepository` | `POST` | Creates a new repository. Required body fields: scm ("git"), is_private (true/false). Optional: project.key (links to a project), description, fork_policy ("no_forks", "no_public_forks", "allow_forks"). ✏️ |
| `updateARepository` | `PUT` | Since this endpoint can be used to both update and to create a |
| `deleteARepository` | `DELETE` | Deletes the repository. This is an irreversible operation. |
| `listCommitsThatModifiedAFile` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of commits that modified the specified file. |
| `listRepositoryForks` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of all the forks of the specified |
| `forkARepository` | `POST` | Creates a new fork of the specified repository. |
| `listWebhooksForARepository` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of webhooks installed on this repository. |
| `createAWebhookForARepository` | `POST` | Creates a new webhook on the specified repository. |
| `getAWebhookForARepository` | `GET` | Returns the webhook with the specified id installed on the specified |
| `updateAWebhookForARepository` | `PUT` | Updates the specified webhook subscription. |
| `deleteAWebhookForARepository` | `DELETE` | Deletes the specified webhook subscription from the given |
| `retrieveTheInheritanceStateForRepositorySettings` | `GET` | Retrieve the inheritance state for repository settings |
| `setTheInheritanceStateForRepositorySettings` | `PUT` | Set the inheritance state for repository settings |
| `listExplicitGroupPermissionsForARepository` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of explicit group permissions for the given repository. |
| `getAnExplicitGroupPermissionForARepository` | `GET` | Returns the group permission for a given group slug and repository |
| `updateAnExplicitGroupPermissionForARepository` | `PUT` | Updates the group permission, or grants a new permission if one does not already exist. |
| `deleteAnExplicitGroupPermissionForARepository` | `DELETE` | Deletes the repository group permission between the requested repository and group, if one exists. |
| `listExplicitUserPermissionsForARepository` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of explicit user permissions for the given repository. |
| `getAnExplicitUserPermissionForARepository` | `GET` | Returns the explicit user permission for a given user and repository. |
| `updateAnExplicitUserPermissionForARepository` | `PUT` | Updates the explicit user permission for a given user and repository. The selected user must be a member of |
| `deleteAnExplicitUserPermissionForARepository` | `DELETE` | Deletes the repository user permission between the requested repository and user, if one exists. |
| `getTheRootDirectoryOfTheMainBranch` | `GET` | This endpoint redirects the client to the directory listing of the |
| `createACommitByUploadingAFile` | `POST` | This endpoint is used to create new commits in the repository by |
| `getFileOrDirectoryContents` | `GET` | Returns the contents of a file or lists a directory. Pass the file path in the `path` query parameter (e.g., path=src/main.go). Omit `path` to list the repo root. Add `at=<branch>` to read a specific branch. File contents are returned as raw text. ✏️ |
| `listRepositoriesWatchers` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of all the watchers on the specified |
| `listRepositoryPermissionsForAUser` | `GET` | **This endpoint is deprecated. Please use the |
| `listRepositoryPermissionsInAWorkspaceForAUser` | `GET` | Returns an object for each repository the caller has explicit access to in the |

## `bitbucket_search`

Search for code across all repositories in a Bitbucket workspace. Use operation searchWorkspace with the search_query parameter.

| Operation | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `searchTeam` | `GET` | Search for code in the repositories of the specified team. |
| `searchAccount` | `GET` | Search for code in the repositories of the specified user. |
| `searchWorkspace` | `GET` | Full-text and path search across all repos in a workspace. search_query examples: ✏️ |

---

| Operation | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `listSnippets` | `GET` | **This endpoint is deprecated. Please use the |
| `createASnippet` | `POST` | Creates a new snippet under the authenticated user's account. |
| `listSnippetsInAWorkspace` | `GET` | Identical to [`/snippets`](/cloud/bitbucket/rest/api-group-snippets/#api-snippets-get), except that the result is further filtered |
| `createASnippetForAWorkspace` | `POST` | Identical to [`/snippets`](/cloud/bitbucket/rest/api-group-snippets/#api-snippets-post), except that the new snippet will be |
| `getASnippet` | `GET` | Retrieves a single snippet. |
| `updateASnippet` | `PUT` | Used to update a snippet. Use this to add and delete files and to |
| `deleteASnippet` | `DELETE` | Deletes a snippet and returns an empty response. |
| `listCommentsOnASnippet` | `GET` | Used to retrieve a paginated list of all comments for a specific |
| `createACommentOnASnippet` | `POST` | Creates a new comment. |
| `getACommentOnASnippet` | `GET` | Returns the specific snippet comment. |
| `updateACommentOnASnippet` | `PUT` | Updates a comment. |
| `deleteACommentOnASnippet` | `DELETE` | Deletes a snippet comment. |
| `listSnippetChanges` | `GET` | Returns the changes (commits) made on this snippet. |
| `getAPreviousSnippetChange` | `GET` | Returns the changes made on this snippet in this commit. |
| `getASnippetsRawFileAtHead` | `GET` | Convenience resource for getting to a snippet's raw files without the |
| `checkIfTheCurrentUserIsWatchingASnippet` | `GET` | Used to check if the current user is watching a specific snippet. |
| `watchASnippet` | `PUT` | Used to start watching a specific snippet. Returns 204 (No Content). |
| `stopWatchingASnippet` | `DELETE` | Used to stop watching a specific snippet. Returns 204 (No Content) |
| `listUsersWatchingASnippet` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of all users watching a specific snippet. |
| `getAPreviousRevisionOfASnippet` | `GET` | Identical to `GET /snippets/encoded_id`, except that this endpoint |
| `updateAPreviousRevisionOfASnippet` | `PUT` | Identical to `UPDATE /snippets/encoded_id`, except that this endpoint |
| `deleteAPreviousRevisionOfASnippet` | `DELETE` | Deletes the snippet. |
| `getASnippetsRawFile` | `GET` | Retrieves the raw contents of a specific file in the snippet. The |
| `getSnippetChangesBetweenVersions` | `GET` | Returns the diff of the specified commit against its first parent. |
| `getSnippetPatchBetweenVersions` | `GET` | Returns the patch of the specified commit against its first |

## `bitbucket_users`

Manage Bitbucket users

| Operation | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `getCurrentUser` | `GET` | Returns the currently logged in user. |
| `listEmailAddressesForCurrentUser` | `GET` | Returns all the authenticated user's email addresses. Both |
| `getAnEmailAddressForCurrentUser` | `GET` | Returns details about a specific one of the authenticated user's |
| `getAUser` | `GET` | Gets the public information associated with a user account. |
| `listGpgKeys` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of the user's GPG public keys. |
| `addANewGpgKey` | `POST` | Adds a new GPG public key to the specified user account and returns the resulting key. |
| `getAGpgKey` | `GET` | Returns a specific GPG public key belonging to a user. |
| `deleteAGpgKey` | `DELETE` | Deletes a specific GPG public key from a user's account. |
| `listSshKeys` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of the user's SSH public keys. |
| `addANewSshKey` | `POST` | Adds a new SSH public key to the specified user account and returns the resulting key. |
| `getASshKey` | `GET` | Returns a specific SSH public key belonging to a user. |
| `updateASshKey` | `PUT` | Updates a specific SSH public key on a user's account |
| `deleteASshKey` | `DELETE` | Deletes a specific SSH public key from a user's account. |

Manage Bitbucket workspaces

| Operation | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `listWorkspacesForTheCurrentUser` | `GET` | **This endpoint is deprecated. Please use the supported alternatives:** |
| `getUserWorkspaces` | `GET` | Returns an object for each workspace accessible to the caller. This object |
| `getUserPermissionOnAWorkspace` | `GET` | Returns the caller's effective role; as in, the highest level of privilege |
| `listWorkspacesForUser` | `GET` | **This endpoint is deprecated. Please use the |
| `getAWorkspace` | `GET` | Returns the requested workspace. |
| `listWebhooksForAWorkspace` | `GET` | Returns a paginated list of webhooks installed on this workspace. |
| `createAWebhookForAWorkspace` | `POST` | Creates a new webhook on the specified workspace. |
| `getAWebhookForAWorkspace` | `GET` | Returns the webhook with the specified id installed on the given |
| `updateAWebhookForAWorkspace` | `PUT` | Updates the specified webhook subscription. |
| `deleteAWebhookForAWorkspace` | `DELETE` | Deletes the specified webhook subscription from the given workspace. |
| `listUsersInAWorkspace` | `GET` | Returns all members of the requested workspace. |
| `getUserMembershipForAWorkspace` | `GET` | Returns the workspace membership, which includes |
| `listUserPermissionsInAWorkspace` | `GET` | Returns the list of members in a workspace |
| `listAllRepositoryPermissionsForAWorkspace` | `GET` | Returns an object for each repository permission for all of a |
| `listARepositoryPermissionsForAWorkspace` | `GET` | Returns an object for the repository permission of each user in the |
| `listProjectsInAWorkspace` | `GET` | Returns the list of projects in this workspace. |
| `createAProjectInAWorkspace` | `POST` | Creates a new project. |
| `getAProjectForAWorkspace` | `GET` | Returns the requested project. |
| `updateAProjectForAWorkspace` | `PUT` | Since this endpoint can be used to both update and to create a |
| `deleteAProjectForAWorkspace` | `DELETE` | Deletes this project. This is an irreversible operation. |
| `listWorkspacePullRequestsForAUser` | `GET` | Returns all workspace pull requests authored by the specified user. |
| `getTheWorkspaceSystemGpgPublicKeys` | `GET` | Returns the system public GPG key(s). In most cases a single key is returned. |
